meanings.definition: behave violently, as if in state of a great anger
be violent; as of fires and storms

examples: Rage against the dying of the light!
The children tormented the stuttering teacher

meanings.definition: cause annoyance in; disturb, especially by minor irritations
examples: Mosquitoes buzzing in my ear really bothers me
It irritates me that she never closes the door after she leaves
rag that old tune

meanings.definition: harass with persistent criticism or carping
examples: The children teased the new teacher
Don't ride me so hard over my failure
His fellow workers razzed him when he wore a jacket and tie
The mother scolded the child for entering a stranger's car
The deputy ragged the Prime Minister
The customer dressed down the waiter for bringing cold soup
rag ore

meanings.definition: characterized by violent and forceful activity or movement; very intense
examples: the fighting became hot and heavy
a hot engagement
a raging battle
the river became a raging torrent
a raging thirst
a raging toothache

meanings.definition: (of the elements) as if showing violent anger
examples: angry clouds on the horizon
furious winds
the raging sea
